implementation_plan: The implementation follows an automated CI/CD pipeline for repository nfcudevopstest on branch refs/heads/main (service_context.source_branch, service_context.repository_name). The high-level sequence is: 1) Initialize and prepare the build, including Checkpoint and Initialize job (raw.timeline.records[0], raw.timeline.records[1]) and resolving the Python environment via Resolve Python and Create CI venv tasks (raw.timeline.records[3], raw.timeline.records[4]). 2) Checkout the main branch source code using Checkout nfcudevopstest@main to s and corresponding post-job cleanup (task_evidence, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[2], raw.timeline.records[6], raw.timeline.records[14], raw.timeline.records[20]). 3) Build and package the application in the Build stage (stage Build: result=succeeded, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[32]) using Package jobs (job_evidence, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[18] and raw.timeline.records[21]) and implementation tasks such as Prepare package, ArchiveFiles, and PublishPipelineArtifact (implementation_signals). The resulting artifact drop is published as a PipelineArtifact (artifact_evidence.name=drop, source_ref=raw.artifacts.value[0]). 4) Execute the Test stage (stage Test: result=succeeded, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[11]) with RunTests jobs (job_evidence, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[7], raw.timeline.records[10]) and the Run tests task (task_evidence, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[8]). 5) Deploy the built artifact to production in the Deploy to Prod stage (stage Deploy to Prod: result=succeeded, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[22]) using DeployWebApp jobs (job_evidence, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[24] and raw.timeline.records[27]) to apply the drop artifact to the target web application. 6) Finalize the pipeline via Finalize build and Report build status tasks (job_evidence.source_ref=raw.timeline.records[33]; task_evidence.source_ref=raw.timeline.records[31]). No explicit manual steps, approvals, or rollback procedures are described in the available evidence.

validation_plan: Post-deployment validation should rely on the existing pipeline stages and tasks as conservative indicators. After the Deploy to Prod stage and DeployWebApp jobs complete (stage Deploy to Prod: result=succeeded, source_ref=raw.timeline.records[22]; job_evidence.source_ref=raw.timeline.records[24], raw.timeline.records[27]), the deployment should be verified by: 1) Confirming that the pipeline completed successfully through the Finalize build job and Report build status task (job_evidence.source_ref=raw.timeline.records[33]; task_evidence.source_ref=raw.timeline.records[31]). 2) Reviewing logs from the DeployWebApp jobs and associated tasks for errors or warnings (job_evidence.source_ref=raw.timeline.records[24], raw.timeline.records[27]). 3) Using the existing Checkpoint and Checkpoint.Authorization signals (validation_signals) as control points to ensure that authorization and pipeline checkpoints completed without issues. Because no detailed functional or user-facing validation steps are present in the evidence and no production health metrics or automated post-deployment tests are shown, additional application-level validation (such as smoke tests, basic user journey checks, and monitoring of key service indicators) should be performed manually or via separate tooling outside the scope of the collected evidence.
